title: Manually re-run flaky e2e smoke suite after every staging deploy
affected_component: staging deploy pipeline / e2e smoke suite

evidence_log.note: Staging deploy #482 smoke suite failed on a known-flaky checkout test; re-ran manually via CI UI, passed second try, deploy proceeded.

manual_procedure: After a staging deploy, if the e2e smoke suite fails, an engineer opens the CI UI, inspects which test failed, and if it matches the known-flaky checkout test, manually clicks 're-run failed jobs'. This is manual because there is no automatic retry policy configured for this specific job, and nobody has confirmed the flake is safe to auto-retry.
proposed_fix: Add a scoped auto-retry (max 1 retry) to the e2e smoke suite CI job config, restricted to the specific known-flaky checkout test by name, with a Slack notification on the retry so it stays visible rather than silently masking a real regression.
falsifiability_gate: If the checkout test does not fail again on staging over the next 2 weeks of deploys, this was a one-time flake, not a recurring pattern, and the entry should be DISQUALIFIED rather than built against.

title: Manually reconstruct DATABASE_URL when exec'ing into the checkout-service container

evidence_log.note: Needed to run a one-off query inside checkout-service container; docker exec spawns a shell with no DATABASE_URL, had to read the secret file and rebuild the connection string by hand.
Same thing again while debugging an order-total mismatch; rebuilt DATABASE_URL by hand a second time, same steps as 5/10.

manual_procedure: docker exec into checkout-service does not inherit the entrypoint's environment, so DATABASE_URL is unset in the exec'd shell. An engineer manually reads the DB password from the mounted secret file and reconstructs postgresql://user:password@db:5432/checkout by hand every time a script or psql session is needed inside the container.
proposed_fix: Add a small `docker exec`-safe wrapper script (e.g. scripts/with_db_env.sh) baked into the image that reads the secret file and exports DATABASE_URL before handing off to the requested command, so engineers run `with_db_env.sh psql ...` instead of reconstructing the URL by hand each time.
falsifiability_gate: If a search of on-call chat history shows this DATABASE_URL reconstruction has not recurred in the last 60 days, treat it as resolved by some other change (e.g. an env-var fix) and re-evaluate before building the wrapper.

title: Manually copy prod feature-flag config into staging before every release rehearsal

evidence_log.note: Release rehearsal for v2.14 required prod flag state in staging; copied 11 flags by hand via the admin UI.
Release rehearsal for v2.15; same manual flag copy, missed one flag the first time, had to redo.
Release rehearsal for v2.16; same manual copy, took ~20 minutes.
Release rehearsal for v2.17; same manual copy again, 4th time in a row across 4 separate release cycles.

manual_procedure: Before each release rehearsal, an engineer opens the feature-flag admin UI for prod, reads the current state of every flag relevant to the release, and manually re-creates that same state in the staging environment's admin UI one flag at a time, because there is no export/import or sync tool between the two environments today.
proposed_fix: Build a small CLI (flagsync) that reads prod flag state via the existing read-only flags API and applies it to staging via the existing write API, with a dry-run diff mode so an engineer can review before applying. Runs as a single command before rehearsal instead of a 20-minute manual UI pass.
falsifiability_gate: If the next 2 release rehearsals do not need a flag copy (e.g. because staging and prod flags are unified some other way), this entry should be DEFERRED rather than built, since the recurring need would have disappeared.

title: Manually tail and grep app logs across 3 hosts to confirm a canary deploy is healthy

evidence_log.note: Canary deploy for v2.9; SSH'd into 3 canary hosts, tailed logs, grepped for ERROR by hand for 10 minutes before promoting.
Canary deploy for v2.10; same manual 3-host log tail.
Canary deploy for v2.11; same manual process, missed a transient error spike because grepping 3 terminals at once is error-prone.
Canary deploy for v2.12; same manual process.
Canary deploy for v2.13; same manual process, 5th time.
Canary deploy for v2.13-hotfix; same manual process, 6th time, decided to build the aggregator this cycle.

manual_procedure: Before promoting a canary deploy, an engineer SSHes into each of the 3 canary hosts, tails the app log, and greps for ERROR/FATAL lines by eye across 3 separate terminal sessions for several minutes, because there was no centralized log aggregation for canary hosts specifically.
proposed_fix: Ship a canary_healthcheck.py script that pulls the last N minutes of logs from all 3 canary hosts via the existing log-shipping API, aggregates and greps centrally, and prints a single pass/fail summary with the offending lines if any, replacing the 3-terminal manual tail.
falsifiability_gate: If canary_healthcheck.py is not actually invoked by engineers on the next 3 canary deploys (i.e. they revert to manual tailing anyway), that is evidence the automation did not actually solve the friction and BUILT should be reconsidered.

post_build_check.procedure: Confirm canary_healthcheck.py was actually invoked (not bypassed) on the next 3 canary deploys after PR-441 merged, by checking CI job logs for the script's output block.

title: Manually restart the payments-webhook consumer after a one-time upstream provider outage

evidence_log.note: Upstream payment provider had a documented 40-minute regional outage; webhook consumer backed up and needed a manual restart once the provider recovered.

DISQUALIFIED on review: provider's own postmortem confirmed this was a one-time regional incident on their side, not a recurring internal pattern. Golden-rule answer was marked true at intake based on an incomplete read of the incident; this was actually one-off forensic/incident work and should have been filed as incident documentation instead. Kept in the log (not deleted) as an audit record of the intake correction.

manual_procedure: Manually restarted the payments-webhook consumer process via the ops console after confirming the upstream provider's status page showed recovery, to clear the backlog of queued retries.
proposed_fix: N/A — disqualified before a fix was scoped; see evidence_log for the correction note.
falsifiability_gate: This entry itself was falsified: the provider's own postmortem confirmed this was a one-time regional incident on their side, not a recurring pattern in our system.
